title: Money and banking
paperName: Cambridge International A Level Economics (P3 & P4)
summary: 9708 A Level — functions of money, commercial banking, credit creation, and central bank role.
learningObjectives: Explain how commercial banks create credit.

simpleExplanation.title: Money in the economy
simpleExplanation.summary: Banks lend deposits, expanding money supply. Central banks set interest rates and act as lender of last resort.
steps: Money: exchange, store of value, unit of account.
Central bank: base rate, open market operations.
QE: asset purchases when rates near zero.

sections.content: **Money and banking** (9.4) supports **monetary policy** essays. Link **interest rates** to **AD** transmission clearly.

sections.label: Mark a money and banking question

steps.caption: Functions of money: medium of exchange, store of value, unit of account.
Commercial banks — fractional reserve, credit creation.
Central bank controls base money and interest rates.
Quantitative easing when conventional policy exhausted.
